topic: Ruff - the Astral Python linter & formatter

questions.q: What is Ruff, what language is it implemented in, and which company maintains it?
Which standalone Python tools does Ruff aim to replace/consolidate?
What is Ruff's default value for lint.select (the rule codes enabled by default)?
What are Ruff's default line-length and indent-width values?
In the lint config, what is the difference between `select`/`ignore` and `extend-select`/`extend-ignore`?
What are Ruff's three config file formats, and what is their precedence when multiple exist in the same directory?
By default, does `ruff check --fix` apply unsafe fixes, and how do you enable unsafe fixes?
For the formatter, what are the default quote-style and indent-style, and how close is Ruff to Black on real-world code?
How do you enable docstring code formatting in Ruff, and what does docstring-code-line-length default to?
What source plugin do each of these Ruff rule prefixes come from: F, E/W, I, UP, B, SIM, S, and RUF?
How does Ruff's versioning policy work - does it follow standard SemVer, and what do minor vs patch version bumps mean?
How is a preview-only lint rule (say HYP001) activated, and does selecting it explicitly or via `select = ["ALL"]` enable it without preview mode?
What command runs Ruff's built-in language server, in which Ruff versions did it reach beta and stable, and what is the official VS Code extension ID?
According to these docs, is Ruff a type checker that can replace mypy/pyright?
